content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN non-negative integers, where NNN is odd. Find the minimum non-negative integer xxx that satisfies the equation

(A1+x)‚äï(A2+x)‚äï‚ãØ‚äï(AN+x)=0(A_1 + x) \oplus (A_2 + x) \oplus \dots \oplus (A_N + x) = 0(A1‚Äã+x)‚äï(A2‚Äã+x)‚äï‚ãØ‚äï(AN‚Äã+x)=0

where ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation. If no such xxx exists, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1.

**Note:** The input of this problem is large, so use fast input/output methods.


Input Format

First line of the input contains a single integer TTT, the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, denoting the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated non-negative integers A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \dots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã‚Ä¶AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer. If there does not exist any non-negative integer xxx that satisfies the given equation, print ‚àí1-1‚àí1. Otherwise, print the minimum value of xxx that satisfies the given equation.
constraints: The value of NNN is guaranteed to be odd.
Sum of NNN over all test cases is less than or equal to 10610^6106.
The value of NNN is guaranteed to be odd.
Sum of NNN over all test cases is less than or equal to 10310^3103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1:
We have to find minimum non-negative integer xxx such that
(2+x)‚äï(4+x)‚äï(5+x)=0(2 + x) \oplus (4 + x) \oplus (5 + x) = 0(2+x)‚äï(4+x)‚äï(5+x)=0
Let f(x)=(2+x)‚äï(4+x)‚äï(5+x)f(x) = (2 + x) \oplus (4 + x) \oplus (5 + x)f(x)=(2+x)‚äï(4+x)‚äï(5+x).

For x=0x = 0x=0, we have
f(0)=(2+0)‚äï(4+0)‚äï(5+0)=2‚äï4‚äï5=3f(0) = (2 + 0) \oplus (4 + 0) \oplus (5 + 0) = 2\oplus 4\oplus 5 = 3f(0)=(2+0)‚äï(4+0)‚äï(5+0)=2‚äï4‚äï5=3
For x=1x = 1x=1, we have
f(1)=(2+1)‚äï(4+1)‚äï(5+1)=3‚äï5‚äï6=0f(1) = (2 + 1) \oplus (4 + 1) \oplus (5 + 1) = 3\oplus 5\oplus 6 = 0f(1)=(2+1)‚äï(4+1)‚äï(5+1)=3‚äï5‚äï6=0

Therefore, x=1x = 1x=1 is the minimum non-negative integer that satisfies the given equation.
Test case 2:
We have to find minimum non-negative integer xxx such that
(0+x)‚äï(0+x)‚äï(0+x)=0(0 + x) \oplus (0 + x) \oplus (0 + x) = 0(0+x)‚äï(0+x)‚äï(0+x)=0
i.e. x‚äïx‚äïx=0x \oplus x \oplus x = 0x‚äïx‚äïx=0. But since 0‚äï0‚äï0=00 \oplus 0 \oplus 0 = 00‚äï0‚äï0=0, it follows that x=0x = 0x=0 is the minimum non-negative integer that satisfies the given equation.
Test case 3:
We have to find minimum non-negative integer xxx such that
(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(1+x)=0(1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) = 0(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(1+x)=0
But a‚äïa=0a \oplus a = 0a‚äïa=0 and 0‚äïa=a0 \oplus a = a0‚äïa=a for any non-negative integer aaa. It follows that
(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(1+x)=0‚äï(1+x)=1+x(1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) = 0 \oplus (1 + x) = 1 + x(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(1+x)=0‚äï(1+x)=1+x
This implies that xxx satisfies the given equation if and only if it satisfies 1+x=01 + x = 01+x=0 if and only if x=‚àí1x = -1x=‚àí1. But ‚àí1-1‚àí1 is a negative integer and therefore, there does not exist any xxx that satisfies the given equation.
Test case 4:
We have to find minimum non-negative integer xxx such that
(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(2+x)=0(1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) \oplus (2 + x) = 0(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(2+x)=0
It follows that
(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(2+x)=0‚äï(2+x)=2+x(1 + x) \oplus (1 + x) \oplus (2 + x) = 0 \oplus (2 + x) = 2 + x(1+x)‚äï(1+x)‚äï(2+x)=0‚äï(2+x)=2+x
This implies that xxx satisfies the given equation if and only if it satisfies 2+x=02 + x = 02+x=0, which can only happen if x=‚àí2x = -2x=‚àí2. But ‚àí2-2‚àí2 is a negative integer and therefore, there does not exist any non-negative xxx that satisfies the given equation, hence we output ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
